what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment record: YAML frontmatter in a Markdown file that registers an image attachment (id att-a972ee22) in the design-source channel, pointing to the generated image target image://design-source/generated/keystone-v3-trial and recording when it was added.
observations: The entire content is YAML frontmatter with no body text.
Fields present: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added.
target uses an image:// URI scheme: image://design-source/generated/keystone-v3-trial.
standouts: The target URI points to a generated image named keystone-v3-trial.
It is typed as an images attachment in the design-source channel.
It carries a precise UTC timestamp for when the attachment was added.
conventions: Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset.
Uses a custom image:// URI scheme for asset targeting.
Attachment filename encodes the id (att-a972ee22.md matches the id value).
concerns: The image target lacks a file extension, so the actual format is not stated.
There is no alt text, caption, or description of the image content.
There is no author, generator, or provenance beyond the timestamp.
No version or trial context is explained in the file itself.
notes: A bare provenance stub that records a generated design image in the design-source channel at a specific point in time.
questions: What does the keystone-v3-trial image depict?
Is this attachment referenced by a specific channel message or design artifact?
What distinguishes v3 of this trial from prior versions?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (no explicit purpose statement is present; the file only declares itself as an image attachment record)
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create a retrievable, timestamped reference to a generated design image so the channel memory system can locate and associate it with design-source activity.
novelty: The target uses an image:// URI scheme rather than a filesystem path, and the asset name keystone-v3-trial suggests a generated, versioned trial artifact without further context in the file.
contribution: It contributes a typed, timestamped pointer to a generated image asset in the design-source channel, giving the fused system a discoverable provenance record for that image.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter Markdown attachment record with id att-a972ee22 in the design-source channel that points to the generated image target image://design-source/generated/keystone-v3-trial and records an added timestamp.
